title: rosa × schottiana var. kosinsciana
intro: Rosa × schottiana var. kosinsciana, also known as the Kosin's rose, is a rare and lesser-known variety of rose plant, belonging to the family Rosaceae. The medicinal properties of the Kosin's rose include its high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic potential. The health benefits of consuming the Kosin's rose include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations. The herbal preparations of the Kosin's rose typically involve infusions, decoctions, or infusions of its flowers, leaves, or roots, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and other medicinal remedies.
